name: Strawberry White Chocolate Mousse Tart
recipeIngredient: 1/4 Cup Firmly Packed Light Brown Sugar
1 3/4 Sticks Unsalted Butter, chilled and cut into pieces
1 Large Egg White, at room temperature
1/2 Cup Whipping Cream, whipped to stiff peaks
recipeInstructions:
For the pastry:
1. Mix flour, brown sugar, orange peel, and salt in a large bowl.
2. Add butter and cut into mixture until it resembles fine meal.
3. Blend orange juice with egg yolk and vanilla.
4. Add enough juice mixture to dry ingredients to form a ball that comes together.
5. Gather dough into a ball and flatten into roughly a 12-inch round.
6. Position rack in the center of the oven and preheat to 375°F. Roll dough out between sheets of plastic wrap to 1/8 inch thickness. Trim to an 11-inch circle (use tart pan as guide).
7. Remove plastic wrap from top and invert into a 10-inch-round springform pan with removable bottom. Remove plastic wrap and press into the bottom and up the sides of the pan, crimping top edges. Freeze for 15 minutes.
8. Line tart shell with aluminum foil and weight with pie weights or beans. Bake until sides are set (about 10 minutes).
9. Remove foil and weights. Bake crust until golden brown (about 16-20 minutes). Sprinkle two ounces of white chocolate over hot crust. Let stand for about 1 minute. Using the back of a spoon, spread chocolate over bottom and up sides. Transfer to rack to cool.
For the mousse:
1. Melt white chocolate with 1/4 cup heavy cream in a small heavy saucepan over very low heat, stirring constantly.
2. Pour into bowl. Let stand until just cool.
3. Beat egg white in a small bowl until soft peaks form. Gradually add sugar and beat until stiff but not dry.
4. Fold whipped cream, 2 tablespoons Grand Marnier, and egg white into chocolate mixture.
5. Spoon mousse into prepared pan, spreading evenly. Refrigerate until mousse is set (2 hours or overnight. Can be made ahead and frozen at this point).
To assemble:
1. Fan strawberries by making several lengthwise cuts in each, starting 1/4 inch from the base and extending through the tip.
2. Fan with fingertips.
3. Bring jam and remaining 2 tablespoons Grand Marnier to a boil in a heavy saucepan, stirring constantly. Transfer to processor and puree.
4. Brush thin layer of jam over mousse. Place 2 fanned strawberries with stems on opposing sides of mousse. Arrange smaller fanned strawberries atop artistically.
